goal: Hub v1 static export: read-only HTML site generator over local synced intent view (validates human-reader value before centralized Hub v2)

summary.title: Hub v1 static export: read-only HTML site over the local intent view
summary.what: New `mainline hub` command family that exports the local synced intent view as a read-only static HTML site. `mainline hub export <dir>` writes the site under <dir>; `mainline hub open` rebuilds at .mainline/hub and opens index.html in the system browser. Pages: recent intents (index), per-intent detail (summary/decisions/rejected/risks/followups/files/related), per-file history (reverse index), per-actor history, risk-heavy intents, supersedes/superseded-by adjacency graph, and a JSON dump of the HubModel. Single embedded stylesheet, no JS, no client-side framework. Architecture splits the package into model.go (durable HubModel — planned to survive as the Hub v2 API DTO contract), export.go and render.go (throwaway), templates/ (header/footer partials + per-page bodies). Hub takes only *storage.Store — no engine import — so engine can later import hub without a cycle.
summary.why: Before paying for a hosted Hub v2 (centralised ingest across repos, search, agent context API, audit views, notifications), validate the cheaper question: does a centralised, browsable view of intent history pull its weight for human readers? Hub v1 is a deliberately throwaway prototype that answers that question without a server, DB, account, or live sync. If yes, v2 is worth building; if no, the static export is killed and the model contract carries forward whatever survives.
summary.user_goal: Let the user open `.mainline/hub/index.html` in a browser and answer 'why is src/auth.go this way?' faster than they can with mainline log/show/trace at the CLI.

decisions.chose: internal/hub depends on *storage.Store only, not engine.Service.
decisions.rationale: Hub v1 only needs read-only view access. Keeping the dependency minimal means engine can later import hub (e.g. for `mainline status --hub-link`) without an import cycle, and it makes the model layer trivial to unit-test.

decisions.chose: model.go + the JSON shape at data/intents.json are the durable contract; export.go and render.go are throwaway.
decisions.rationale: Hub v2 hosted will replace the static-site renderer with an HTTP API. The model layer is what becomes the API DTO. By keeping derivation pure (no I/O in buildHubModel) and the JSON dump round-tripping, the cost of pivoting is bounded to throwing away templates + filesystem layout.

decisions.chose: Skip turn timeline at export time; surface a `mainline trace <id>` CLI hint instead.
decisions.rationale: Trace requires reading actor logs per intent, which would scale poorly at export time as intent count grows. The CLI hint gets the user to the same data without making export expensive.

decisions.chose: Replace `/` with `__` and unsafe chars with `_`. Theoretical collisions accepted.
decisions.rationale: v2 hosted will move to API URLs (?path=src/auth.go). v1 is a static-file site, so paths must be filesystem-safe; collision risk is real but the surface is per-repo and small. v2 makes this concern moot.

decisions.chose: header/footer partials, each page is its own root template.
decisions.rationale: Initial attempt with shared `body` blocks across pages failed because Go templates take the last-parsed definition globally. Header/footer split avoids the collision and keeps each page self-contained.

rejected.alternative: Skip Hub v1 and jump straight to hosted v2.
rejected.reason: Premature investment. v2 only earns its keep if the centralised-view question is answered yes; v1 answers it for the cost of one Go package + html/template.

rejected.alternative: Embed the trace timeline (turn-by-turn) on each intent detail page.
rejected.reason: Requires reading actor logs per intent at export time. Pushes export from O(intents) to O(intents × turns) and bloats the static site. CLI link is good enough for v1.

rejected.alternative: Use a JS framework / client-side router.
rejected.reason: Hub v1 is a 30-day validation, not a UI investment. Plain HTML/CSS keeps the build trivial and the renderer throwaway.

Those APIs format for CLI output (Log paginates, Show branches on draft vs view). Hub wants a flat enumeration of all sealed intents, which is one ReadMainlineView call. Less code, fewer formatting concerns.

risks: fileSlug collisions (e.g. `a__b.go` vs `a/b.go`) are theoretically possible. Acceptable for v1 because v2 moves to API URLs; flagged in code comment.

Trace timeline absent on intent detail pages may make Hub feel less rich than mainline trace. Mitigation: CLI hint, plus the user's three dogfood questions (would you actually open hub? does file page beat CLI? does risk view help review?) explicitly cover the validation we want, not feature parity with the CLI.
Hub v1 has no auth and writes to user-chosen paths. Defensive: only writes inside the explicit OutputDir; never modifies repo files.
followups: Dogfood for 1–2 weeks; specifically answer: do I open hub? does file page beat CLI? does risk view help review/audit?
If yes → start Hub v2 design (hosted, ingest from git remote, search, agent-context API).
If no → kill the package; the model contract may still seed a richer JSON export via `mainline log --json`.
If kept long-term: add `--limit` / `--since` to bound export size on long-lived repos.
Consider minimal client-side filtering on index.html (status/actor) once the page exceeds ~200 intents and table-sort UX matters.

architectural_claims: Hub v1 is a throwaway prototype; only HubModel and the data/intents.json shape are part of the durable contract that Hub v2 will inherit.
Hub depends on storage.Store, not engine.Service, to avoid a future import cycle when engine wants to link to hub.
Page derivation is pure: buildHubModel takes a *MainlineView and produces a HubModel with no I/O, so the model layer is unit-testable in isolation.
behavioral_changes: New CLI subtree: `mainline hub export <dir>` and `mainline hub open`.
First export creates 5 page types + JSON dump + assets; idempotent within an export run (re-running overwrites).
`hub open` defaults to .mainline/hub and shells out to `open` (macOS), `xdg-open` (Linux), or rundll32 (Windows).

api_changes.signature: hub.HubModel / hub.HubIntent / hub.HubFileEntry / hub.HubActorEntry / hub.HubRelationRow JSON contract

data_model_changes.name: HubModel JSON dump at <out>/data/intents.json
data_model_changes.migration_notes: New artefact; consumed only by Hub v1 templates today. Survives into Hub v2 as the planned API DTO shape.

security_implications: Hub never writes outside the user-chosen OutputDir. No network IO.
Static site has no JS / no client-side fetch — XSS surface is limited to text fields rendered through html/template auto-escaping (default ON).
`hub open` shells out to system `open`/`xdg-open` with a static, code-controlled path. No untrusted input.
